page: UpCloud GPU-24xCPU-240GB-1xB200 is a GPU-accelerated virtual server powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 24 vCPUs mapped to 24 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8570 processor running at 2.0 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The CPU allocation is shared, and the processor includes advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for tile and int8 operations. The system is equipped with 240.0 GB of system memory, providing 10.0 GB of RAM per core.

Acceleration is driven by a single NVIDIA Blackwell B200 GPU featuring 192 GB of VRAM. This server does not include local storage, meaning storage must be provisioned externally. Network connectivity includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. The combination of the Blackwell GPU architecture and high-capacity system memory makes this instance highly capable for memory-intensive parallel computing, though the shared CPU allocation represents a potential tradeoff for workloads requiring sustained, exclusive physical CPU cores.

Benchmark data highlights top-tier performance in LLM inference, with a small 135M model achieving 9953.26 tokens per second for prompt processing and 525.92 tokens per second for text generation. Memory bandwidth is strong, reaching 4156.98 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 251.17 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. Passmark CPU testing yields a strong score of 52754.5, supported by strong database operations and static web serving throughput. This server is optimized for deep learning, LLM inference, data science, and high-performance computing workloads.
description: UpCloud GPU-24xCPU-240GB-1xB200 is a high-performance, GPU-accelerated virtual server built on the KVM hypervisor. It features 24 shared vCPUs from an Intel Xeon 8570 processor, 240.0 GB of system memory, and a single NVIDIA Blackwell B200 GPU with 192 GB of VRAM. The server lacks local storage, requiring external storage solutions, but includes one public IPv4 address. Benchmark results show top-tier LLM inference speeds, alongside strong memory bandwidth and Passmark CPU scores. This hardware profile is highly suited for deep learning, artificial intelligence inference, and complex data science workloads.
og_description: UpCloud GPU-24xCPU-240GB-1xB200 is a KVM-based virtual server featuring 24 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 240 GB RAM, and an NVIDIA Blackwell B200 GPU with 192 GB VRAM, optimized for LLM inference and deep learning.
meta_description: UpCloud GPU-24xCPU-240GB-1xB200 features 24 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 240 GB RAM, and an NVIDIA B200 GPU with 192 GB VRAM for advanced AI workloads.
tagline: A high-capacity GPU instance featuring Blackwell architecture and extensive system memory for demanding artificial intelligence and deep learning workloads.
bullet_points: NVIDIA Blackwell B200 GPU with 192 GB VRAM for parallel computing
24 Intel Xeon 8570 vCPUs with AVX-512 and AMX instruction sets
240 GB of system memory providing 10 GB of RAM per core
Top-tier LLM inference performance for prompt and text generation
No local storage included, requiring external volume attachment
Best suited for deep learning, AI model inference, and data science
